input.background: Suppose you know a household owns a cat.
input.question: What is the probability that it also owns a dog? In other words, what is the probability of dog ownership given cat ownership?

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性
output.answer: $\operatorname{Pr}(B \mid A)=\frac{\operatorname{Pr}(A \text { and } B)}{\operatorname{Pr}(A)}=\frac{0.15}{0.35}=0.4286$. The probability of owning a dog given cat ownership is about $43 \%$.

Cat_Ownership.description: Indicates whether a household owns a cat.

Dog_Ownership.description: Indicates whether a household owns a dog.

meta_info.caption: Conditional probability (Pet ownership).
meta_info.section: 5.5 MORE RULES AND PROPERTIES OF Probability
